mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 3.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases net present value of corporate cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 3.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 4.5%, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both decline month-over-month simultaneously, indicating synchronized demand destruction across consumer and construction sectors that signals earnings compression and equity repricing downward over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline >2% in same month
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns in >55% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) grows above 102.5 (current level) indicating robust supply-side capacity utilization that supports margin expansion and positive earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth fails to precede positive 5-day returns in >52% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 327 combined with FEDFUNDS held above 3.5% signals the Fed maintaining restrictive policy stance that constrains equity valuations through elevated discount rates and reduced real purchasing power, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction effect between inflation and rates is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100,000 net additions month-over-month, signaling labor market cooling that precedes consumer income weakness and reduced equity earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 48% of instances when PAYEMS growth < 100,000, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth trajectory combined with housing starts (HOUST) above 1,200 thousand units indicates sustained demand expansion and construction investment that supports equity valuations and produces positive 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1200 AND GDPC1 growth positive in latest quarter
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between GDPC1 and HOUST is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining below 102.0 combined with unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.3% signals simultaneous supply contraction and labor market weakness that compresses earnings and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction term between INDPRO and UNRATE is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).
